issues.problem: Could be more specific about the return type. The actual return is `Function2[T, U, BoxedUnit]`, so mentioning that it returns a side-effecting function (Unit/BoxedUnit) would help readers.
Doesn't indicate the result is a Boolean-returning function. Could be more informative.

issues.current_text: the type of the first (object) argument to the consumer
issues.problem: Good clarification that T is the object (non-primitive) argument. Consistent across ObjDoubleConsumer/ObjIntConsumer/ObjLongConsumer. Minor: could also mention the second argument is a primitive for completeness.
issues.suggestion: the type of the first (object) argument to the consumer (the second argument is a primitive)

general_feedback: The documentation is consistent, accurate, and follows a uniform pattern across all ~100+ conversion methods. Each entry correctly identifies type parameters with their semantic role (input/output/return), and @param/@return entries follow Scaladoc conventions (lowercase start, backticked code references). The repetition of the boxed-type note for primitive-specialized converters is appropriate since each method is a standalone API entry point. Documentation could be slightly more informative about return type specifics (e.g., that Predicate-derived functions return Boolean), but this is minor since the method signature is visible. No factual errors, no inconsistencies in style or terminology. File is marked as GENERATED CODE which explains the uniformity — any further improvements would need to be made in the generator.
